artifacts_at_exit.notes: Reused a pre-existing, already-validated, parked change instead of re-proposing (avoids duplicate/bundled scope). Scope = sub-deliverable 1 only (fixture-only Codex/Claude-Code adapter conformance); dogfood activation deliberately excluded here, to be proposed as a separate change (pl-dogfood-activation) after this slice ships, per G1 threat-model's fixture-first sequencing. spectra validate: pass.
Not committed to master directly by mistake -- caught before Review and moved onto codex/pldd-consumer-adapter-parity, excluding a pre-existing unrelated uncommitted README.md change from the commit.

Adversarial subagent found 4 real findings (path traversal via descriptor entry, asymmetric containment in installAdapter, duplicate-host silent parity bypass, case-sensitive boundary regex). All 4 fixed, 5 new tests added, commit b3714eb. Full gstack multi-specialist review army not run -- not configured in this repo; single adversarial pass substituted.

First Stryker run scored 47.23% -- root cause traced (not a tooling artifact as first suspected; genuine missing tests for loadManifest/loadDescriptor error branches, and the CLI main block was only reachable via subprocess spawnSync which Stryker cannot instrument). Fixed by (1) adding tests for every loadManifest/loadDescriptor throw branch and normalizeObservation's malformed-JSON fallback, (2) refactoring the CLI into a pure exported runCli(argv) function callable in-process, with in-process tests for every branch. Re-ran: 68.33% >= 60% break threshold. Remaining survivors/no-coverage are the isMainModule process-entry guard itself (lines 281-317, only exercisable by actually running the file as a subprocess -- same class of irreducible gap Phase A's own CLI blocks had) plus some low-value stdout-formatting mutants not pursued further given threshold met. npm audit --omit=dev: 0 vulnerabilities. Dependency tree unchanged (0 new deps).

think.notes: Scope expanded 2026-07-20 (ctony): dogfood activation in scope. Fixture-first, then live install into agenticToolset; only activation step may write outside src/*.

plan.notes: Slice 1 of 2 (see phase_history.plan.artifacts_at_exit.notes). Slice 2 (pl-dogfood-activation) not yet proposed -- comes after this slice ships.

build.notes: src/scripts/pl-adapter-conformance.mjs + Codex/Claude Code descriptors+templates + fixture manifest (6 cases) + docs. src-only scope guard clean.

review.notes: 4 findings fixed (see report.md), tests pass after fixes, type-check clean.

test.notes: 216/216 tests pass, type-check clean, src-only scope guard clean, npm audit clean. See phase_history.test.artifacts_at_exit.notes for the mutation-testing investigation.

G1.notes: Covers both fixture-only adapters and the dogfood-activation sub-deliverable. Dogfood is the first PLDD write outside src/* -- gated by manifest+snapshot+atomic-apply+tested-rollback per g1-threat-model.md. Open items (scope-guard allowlist mechanism, activation task tracking shape) deferred to Plan, not blocking G1.

checklist_results.owasp_top_10_per_category: n/a -- fixture-only local dev tooling, no network/auth/LLM surface
checklist_results.rate_limiting_circuit_breakers: n/a -- single manual invocation, no auto-retry

G2.notes: Mutation score 68.33% >= 60% threshold (real tests, traced and fixed an initial 47.23% score rather than lowering the bar). 2 real security bugs found and fixed during Review (path traversal via descriptor entry, duplicate-host silent parity bypass). 216/216 tests pass, 0 dependency vulns, src-only scope guard clean. Rollback path verified by baselineHash before/after assertions in both unit tests and the full runConformance suite. Human approved.
